title: Empire biscuits
subtitle: Scottish sandwich biscuits with jam and water icing
excerpt: Scottish double-layered shortbread biscuits sandwiched with raspberry jam, topped with white water icing, and finished with a glacé cherry. A Scottish bakery staple. Makes about fourteen.
sourceNotes: Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): jam-sandwiched iced biscuits are documented in the Scottish baking chapter as a traditional tearoom preparation. The name 'empire biscuit' is a post-First World War renaming of the 'German biscuit' — recorded in Scottish newspaper archives from the 1910s onward. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: iced sandwich biscuits using plain pastry dough and fruit jam are recorded as a standard British domestic preparation.

recipe.batchNotes: Double and bake on two trays.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps in an airtight tin for up to 5 days. Set the icing fully before storing.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf; pale gold only — brown biscuits will crack rather than sand when bitten

content.text: Empire biscuits are a Scottish bakery standard — a double shortbread round sandwiched with jam and topped with a flat sheet of water icing and one glacé cherry. The biscuit base is slightly richer than plain shortbread; a small egg yolk keeps it tender and helps the rounds hold their shape when sandwiched.

Rub the {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} until the mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add the {{egg-yolks}} and work in until the dough comes together. Press into a flat disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Preheat the oven to 160°C fan. Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface to 4–5 mm thick and cut rounds with a 6 cm cutter, re-rolling the scraps once. Space on lined trays and bake for 12–14 minutes until pale gold. Cool completely on a rack before sandwiching.

Spread a thin layer of {{raspberry-jam}} on the flat side of half the biscuits and press the remaining biscuits on top. Make the water icing by mixing the remaining {{icing-sugar}} with just enough cold water to form a thick, spreadable paste (start with 2 tsp and add drops until the icing holds its shape but levels out slowly). Spread over each sandwiched biscuit and press a halved {{glace-cherries}} into the centre. Leave for 30 minutes until the icing is fully set.
